ink.$description: Ink — Primary text, display headlines, full-bleed dark media blocks, nav wordmark — the single near-black that anchors every interface

paper.$description: Paper — Default canvas, card surfaces, text on dark sections

fog.$description: Fog — Subtle surface differentiation for alternating sections or inset panels — barely visible, used to create quiet separation without borders

stone.$description: Stone — Secondary text, category labels, decorative ampersand, muted button text — the entire chromatic-like hierarchy comes from this one gray

graphite.$description: Graphite — Muted body text, helper copy, tertiary metadata — deeper than Stone for occasional emphasis on small text

bentonsans.$description: All UI and body text — navigation links, body copy, footer, buttons, tags, small uppercase eyebrows. A custom grotesque that reads neutral and workmanlike; the substitute should preserve its clean even stroke and wide x-height.

aftenscreen.$description: Display and editorial headlines only. Line-height collapses to 0.80 at the largest sizes — the letters of "Design" and "Technology" nearly touch, creating a condensed mass of ink. Weight stays at 400; scale alone provides emphasis. The tight tracking (-0.05em to -0.04em) tightens the optical spacing so oversized type doesn't feel airy.

paper.$description: Surface level 0: Default page canvas — the overwhelming majority of the interface

fog.$description: Surface level 1: Quiet surface for alternating sections or subtle card differentiation

ink.$description: Surface level 2: Full-bleed media blocks, dark inversions, footer or featured content panels
